input.plan:
# Public SEO Teaser Plan

## Recommendation
Build a **public teaser landing** at `/` and move the current authenticated board behind a clear private route such as `/app`.

This fits the current system better than a full public catalog because:
- the existing board is deeply tied to authenticated per-user state via [`relocation_jobs/web/routes/board.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/routes/board.py)
- the root shell currently boots into login-first behavior from [`relocation_jobs/static/index.html`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/index.html) and [`relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js)
- the backend already has reusable catalog-wide aggregates via [`relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py) and admin composition via [`relocation_jobs/admin/service.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/admin/service.py)

## Target UX
### Public surface
Create a public homepage that shows:
- hero with the product promise: visa-friendly software roles across supported countries
- trust strip with public stats: total companies, total jobs, visa jobs, supported countries, latest updates
- sample job/company preview cards from the catalog, clearly labeled as a preview
- country blocks for Germany, Netherlands, UK, Portugal with job counts and latest fetch freshness
- explanation of what logged-in users get: tracking, apply history, company workspace, admin/fetch tools
- CTA split: `Browse preview` and `Sign in`

### Private surface
Keep the existing authenticated board behavior, but make it a private product area:
- `/app` or equivalent authenticated board shell
- login/register entry from the public homepage
- no change to private board workflows in phase 1

## Information architecture
```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Visitor[Visitor] --> Home[PublicHome]
  Home --> Preview[PublicPreviewData]
  Home --> SignIn[LoginEntry]
  SignIn --> App[PrivateBoard]
  App --> Tracking[PerUserTracking]
  App --> Workspace[CompanyWorkspace]
  App --> Admin[AdminTools]

  PublicApi[PublicReadApi] --> CatalogOverview[CatalogOverview]
  PublicApi --> PreviewFeed[PreviewJobsOrCompanies]
  PrivateApi[PrivateApi] --> UserBoard[AuthenticatedBoardApi]
```

## Backend plan
### 1. Add a public read surface
Introduce a dedicated public route module, likely under [`relocation_jobs/web/routes/`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/routes/), instead of weakening auth on the existing board routes.

Expose only safe read models such as:
- `GET /api/public/overview`
- `GET /api/public/preview`
- optional `GET /api/public/countries`

These should reuse or wrap:
- `get_catalog_overview()` from [`relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py)
- existing stats composition patterns from [`relocation_jobs/admin/service.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/admin/service.py)

Keep this intentionally limited:
- no per-user tracking fields
- no admin/fetch state
- no mutation routes
- no MCP/application workspace data

### 2. Keep `/api/board` private
Do **not** make [`/api/board` in `relocation_jobs/web/routes/board.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/routes/board.py) public in phase 1.

Reason:
- it expects `g.user_id`
- it merges per-user state
- opening it publicly would couple SEO work to board/business-rule cleanup

### 3. Add SEO basics
Add server-served public SEO artifacts:
- meaningful public `<title>` and `<meta name="description">`
- `robots.txt`
- `sitemap.xml` for the public homepage only in phase 1
- canonical URL handling for the homepage
- optional JSON-LD for `WebSite`/`Organization` and a simple collection-style description

These belong near [`relocation_jobs/web/server.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/server.py) and the new public shell/template.

## Frontend plan
### 4. Split the current root experience
Today `/` serves [`relocation_jobs/static/index.html`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/index.html), and [`relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js) immediately checks auth and shows the login gate.

Change the shell strategy to:
- `/` = public landing shell
- `/app` = existing private board shell (reusing the current board experience with minimal churn)

That is the smallest UX change that solves discoverability without a broad rewrite.

### 5. Public homepage structure
Use the existing visual system only as a bridge; do not over-invest in the current monolithic shell.

Recommended sections:
- Hero
- Country stats strip
- Preview cards
- “How it works” in 3 steps
- “What unlocks after sign-in”
- Footer with SEO copy and links

## Design direction
### Recommended design for this system
Given the repo’s current size and mixed frontend stack, the right design is **not** a flashy recruitment marketplace or full consumer job board yet. It should be a **product landing page in front of a private operations/workspace app**.

That means:
- light-first public landing for readability and SEO trust
- card-based preview grid for jobs/companies
- compact stat chips and country sections
- restrained visuals, not heavy illustration
- private app can remain denser and more dashboard-like

### Pinterest references
Use these as reference patterns, not direct copies:
- Public landing / recruitment-site framing: [Recruitment Website Design](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/recruitment-website-design/934085641730/)
- Job-card and listing-card treatment: [Job Listing Card UI Design](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/job-listing-card-ui-design/954364033927/)
- Filter/chip behavior inspiration for future public browse UX: [Filtering & Chip System Wireframe](https://uk.pinterest.com/pin/filtering-chip-system-wireframe-ux-iteration--676736281525582761/)

### Concrete UI recommendation
For this project, I recommend:
- **Public site:** soft light background, strong typography, 1 primary accent, stat chips, country sections, clean card previews
- **Private app:** keep denser dashboard patterns and richer filters behind auth
- **Navigation:** simple top nav with `Preview`, `How it works`, `Sign in`
- **Cards:** company name, country, open-role count, visa indicator, freshness timestamp, maybe 1-2 sample titles
- **Copy:** emphasize curation and relocation focus instead of generic “job search portal” language

## File-level implementation map
Likely touch points:
- [`relocation_jobs/web/server.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/server.py) for public shell routing and SEO files
- new public route module under [`relocation_jobs/web/routes/`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/web/routes/)
- [`relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py) only if a small public-preview query/helper is needed
- [`relocation_jobs/static/index.html`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/index.html) replaced or repurposed as the public landing shell
- new private board shell or route alias for the current authenticated app
- [`relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js) adapted so auth-gated boot moves to the private route instead of the homepage
- focused route/UI tests under [`tests/web/`](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/tests/web/)

## Phasing
### Phase 1
- public homepage
- public overview endpoint
- public preview endpoint
- move private board to `/app`
- add `robots.txt` + homepage sitemap entry

### Phase 2
- polish homepage copy/design
- richer country sections
- optional public company teaser pages if you want more SEO later

### Explicit non-goals for now
- do not make full `/api/board` public
- do not expose user/application workspace data publicly
- do not start the full SPA rewrite as part of this change
- do not build full job-detail SEO pages yet

## Success criteria
- anonymous visitors can understand the product and see real catalog signals
- Google can crawl a real public homepage with job-related content
- authenticated users still get the existing board behavior with minimal disruption
- the architecture stays additive and reversible for a later public-catalog release

Add phase-1 SEO artifacts: title/description, robots.txt, sitemap.xml, canonical handling
Add focused route and anonymous-access tests for the new public homepage and public API surface

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+from relocation_jobs.catalog.repo import get_catalog_overview, load_catalog_companies_page
+
+PREVIEW_LIMIT = 8
+PREVIEW_JOB_LIMIT = 2
+
+
+def _preview_job(job: dict) -> dict:
+    return {
+        "title": job.get("title"),
+        "url": job.get("url"),
+        "visa_sponsorship": job.get("visa_sponsorship"),
+        "fetched": job.get("fetched"),
+    }
+
+
+def _preview_company(company: dict) -> dict:
+    jobs = list(company.get("matching_jobs") or [])
+    preview_jobs = jobs[:PREVIEW_JOB_LIMIT]
+    visa_jobs = sum(1 for job in jobs if job.get("visa_sponsorship") is True)
+    return {
+        "name": company.get("name"),
+        "country": company.get("country"),
+        "country_label": company.get("country_label"),
+        "city": company.get("city"),
+        "locations": company.get("locations") or [],
+        "ats_type": company.get("ats_type"),
+        "careers_url": company.get("careers_url"),
+        "latest_fetched": company.get("latest_fetched"),
+        "job_count": len(jobs),
+        "visa_job_count": visa_jobs,
+        "preview_jobs": [_preview_job(job) for job in preview_jobs],
+    }
+
+
+def _public_overview_payload() -> dict:
+    overview = get_catalog_overview()
+    return {
+        "has_data": overview.get("has_data", False),
+        "countries": overview.get("countries") or [],
+        "totals": overview.get("totals") or {},
+        "country_meta": overview.get("country_meta") or [],
+    }
+
+
+def _public_preview_payload(limit: int = PREVIEW_LIMIT) -> dict:
+    overview = get_catalog_overview()
+    countries = [row.get("country") for row in overview.get("countries") or [] if row.get("country")]
+    company_rows = load_catalog_companies_page(countries, offset=0, limit=limit) if countries else []
+    companies = [_preview_company(company) for _, company in company_rows]
+    return {
+        "companies": companies,
+        "meta": {
+            "limit": limit,
+            "returned": len(companies),
+        },
+    }
+
+
+def register(app):
+    @app.get("/api/public/overview")
+    def api_public_overview():
+        return _public_overview_payload()
+
+    @app.get("/api/public/preview")
+    def api_public_preview():
+        return _public_preview_payload()
*** End Patch

input.plan:
# Relocation Jobs UI Direction Plan

## Design goal
Create a UI that feels like a **curated relocation-job discovery product with a private operator workspace behind it**, not a generic job-board template and not a pure admin dashboard.

## Recommended design direction
### Public surface
The public-facing experience should follow a **clean search/directory pattern**:
- light-first visual design
- strong typography and whitespace
- prominent search and curated preview blocks
- card/list results instead of heavy dashboard chrome
- restrained use of accent color

### Private surface
The signed-in product should follow a **focused SaaS workspace pattern**:
- denser controls and richer filters
- compact stats and status chips
- more utility-first layout around board, company workspace, and admin tools
- same brand tokens as public pages, but with higher information density

## Pinterest references to anchor the direction
### Primary references
- Public search/listing direction: [Search Page UI Design](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/search-page-ui-design/899976332604/)
- Private filters/workspace direction: [Filter Dashboard UI](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/filter-dashboard-ui/905521830181/)

### Secondary references
- Private SaaS shell and workspace patterns: [SaaS Dashboard Ideas](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/saas-dashboard-ideas/945043766924/)
- Directory-like card/list structure: [Employee Directory UI](https://www.pinterest.com/ideas/employee-directory-ui/918205268157/)
- Advanced filter behavior: [Advanced Search / Filter UI](https://www.pinterest.com/astbsljanethui/advanced-search-filter-ui/)

## Product framing
The product is really two connected experiences:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Visitor[Visitor] --> PublicPreview[PublicPreview]
  PublicPreview --> SearchBrowse[SearchAndBrowse]
  PublicPreview --> SignIn[SignIn]
  SignIn --> PrivateBoard[PrivateBoard]
  PrivateBoard --> CompanyWorkspace[CompanyWorkspace]
  PrivateBoard --> AdminTools[AdminTools]
```

That means the UI should be designed as one system with two densities:
- **browse density** for public visitors
- **work density** for signed-in users

## Visual system recommendation
### Brand feel
Use a visual language that communicates:
- curated
- trustworthy
- international
- practical
- not flashy

### Color strategy
- base: neutral light background for public pages
- accent: one primary blue or blue-violet brand color
- semantic colors: success, warning, danger, visa-highlight
- avoid too many gradients and avoid startup-style neon treatments

### Typography
- Inter remains a good fit
- larger headline scale on public pages
- tighter, more compact UI type scale in the private app
- clear hierarchy between title, metadata, status, and filters

### Spacing and surfaces
- public: larger section spacing, more breathable cards
- private: tighter spacing, compact controls, clearer information grouping
- use subtle borders and soft elevation instead of dramatic shadows

## Public experience plan
### Homepage / preview structure
The public page should be redesigned as a **discovery landing page**, not a thin marketing page.

Recommended sections:
1. Hero with search intent and product promise
2. Country coverage / trust stats
3. Curated company or role preview cards
4. How the product helps relocation-focused job search
5. CTA into sign-in/private workspace

### Public UI patterns
Use references from search/directory patterns for:
- search bar placement
- filter chip presentation
- result-card layout
- section rhythm
- empty-state and preview-state treatment

### What to avoid
- generic “startup SaaS” hero art
- giant illustration-heavy landing page
- over-promising public features that don’t exist yet
- dashboard widgets on the public page that feel admin-like

## Private experience plan
### Main board
The private board should remain the operational core, but visually evolve toward a cleaner workspace.

Recommended improvements:
- unify filter row, search, and stats into a more structured control band
- simplify visual weight of secondary buttons
- tighten company card hierarchy so title, counts, and actions scan faster
- separate global board controls from company/job actions more clearly

### Company workspace and admin pages
Follow the SaaS/workspace references for:
- side navigation or anchored section navigation
- cleaner subheaders
- consistent cards and tables
- status chips and activity panels

## UI component plan
Create a shared design language for both public and private surfaces.

### Core components
- Buttons: primary, secondary, ghost
- Chips: country, visa, ATS, status
- Cards: preview card, company card, stat card, workspace card
- Inputs: search, select, filter chip, segmented control
- Layout blocks: hero band, filter toolbar, section header, empty state

### Consistency rule
A public preview card and a private company card should feel related, even if the private one is denser.

## File-level implementation direction
Most likely implementation surfaces if this design work is approved:
- public shell/templates under [relocation_jobs/static/]( /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/ )
- current private board shell in [relocation_jobs/static/index.html](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/index.html)
- private board behavior in [relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/js/main.js)
- private board styling in [relocation_jobs/static/styles.css](/Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/relocation_jobs/static/styles.css)
- React islands / board presentation in [frontend/]( /Users/amin/dev/p-projects/armenia/resume/frontend/ ) if the redesign reaches card rendering or pagination visuals

## Suggested implementation phases
### Phase 1: define the visual system
- choose color palette, spacing scale, type scale
- define public vs private density rules
- define canonical button, chip, and card styles

### Phase 2: redesign the public page
- rebuild the public preview experience around search/directory patterns
- keep copy factual and product-led
- ensure the page visually relates to the signed-in app

### Phase 3: align the private board
- refine header, filter toolbar, cards, and control hierarchy
- reduce visual clutter without changing workflows

### Phase 4: extend to workspace/admin
- apply the same design system to company workspace and admin surfaces
- keep admin pages utilitarian, but visually coherent with the rest of the product

## Recommendation
If only one thing is done first, do this:
- **design the public preview as a clean directory/search page**
- **keep the private app as the denser workspace**
- **unify both with one brand system**

That is the best fit for the actual product shape and the strongest use of the Pinterest references.
